title: Von Neumann self-reproducing automaton conditions applied to swarm
human_signal: john von neumann for the swarm
expect: Genesis bundle insufficient per von Neumann description-complexity theorem; maps to 80/100 daughter score
actual: Boot-tier inequality HOLDS (ratio 1.13) but fixed-point FAILS — genesis_extract.py not in boot tier. One-generation reproduction, not recursive. Predicted swarmability 100 vs actual 80 reveals fixed-point is the binding constraint, not complexity.
diff: Expected complexity deficit; actual explanation is MISSING COPIER (fixed-point failure). The 20-point gap is not about K(D) but about B not in D.

von_neumann_mapping.A_universal_constructor: orient.py + validate_beliefs.py + compact.py + sync_state.py + dispatch_optimizer.py + open_lane.py + close_lane.py + maintenance.py
von_neumann_mapping.B_copier: cell_blueprint.py (IN boot tier) + genesis_extract.py (NOT in boot tier)
von_neumann_mapping.dual_use: D is READ by orient.py (construction) and COPIED by cell_blueprint.py (inheritance) — but genesis_extract.py (the extractor that produces D) is missing from D itself

key_findings: Boot-tier K(D) > K(A+B+C) by 12.8% — complexity inequality satisfied
Fixed-point FAILS: genesis_extract.py not in boot tier → daughter cannot produce granddaughter
This is precisely von Neumann's insight: copier must be in description for recursive self-reproduction
The 80/100 swarmability is NOT an information deficit — it's a missing-copier deficit
Full swarm is 48.5x larger than boot description (2.1% coverage) — growth requires external input
Minimax: swarm under-falsifies by ~32x (2.8% vs 90.9% at 10:1 cost asymmetry)

prescription.fix_fixed_point: Add genesis_extract.py to boot tier → enables recursive self-reproduction
prescription.minimax_calibration: 10:1 cost ratio is starting assumption. Even at 3:1, optimal rate is 75% vs current 2.8%
prescription.von_neumann_stability: Future: apply vN stability analysis to discrete session-update dynamics (F-MATH9)
